name: Nathan Davis
abstract: Nathan Tate Davis (February 15, 1937 – April 8, 2018) was an American jazz multi-instrumentalist who played the tenor saxophone, soprano saxophone, bass clarinet, and flute. He is known for his work with Eric Dolphy, Kenny Clarke, Ray Charles, Slide Hampton and Art Blakey.

about_quotes.speaker: David N. Baker
about_quotes.evidence: I made an album with Toots Thielemans and Nathan Davis called Gettin’ It Together
about_quotes.doc_title: David N. Baker — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Wilbur Ware
about_quotes.evidence: He had a job for me up at Amherst. ... He had Nathan Davis, Dr. Nathan Davis, from the University of Pittsburgh on tenor.
about_quotes.doc_title: 68088_Wilbur B. Ware, Sr oral history interview

about_quotes.speaker: Chico Freeman
about_quotes.evidence: I also have worked with the band called Roots... And then there’s Benny Golson, Arthur Blythe, Nathan Davis and myself.
about_quotes.doc_title: Chico Freeman interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 13, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: JJ Johnson
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] I believe he is. I asked Nathan Davis. Nathan said he doesn't know what he’s doing, but that he's alive.
about_quotes.doc_title: JJ Johnson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Winard Harper
about_quotes.evidence: guys like Nathan Davis, some of those pioneers fought for and helped put jazz education in place
about_quotes.doc_title: Winard Harper interviewed by Monk Rowe, Utica, New York, October 10, 2014

about_quotes.speaker: James Moody
about_quotes.evidence: Well there’s a friend of mine who was the provost at the University of Pittsburgh. And he’s a very good friend of Dr. Nathan Davis... Well a book is being written about me, by Nathan Davis’ wife, Dr. Ursula Davis.
about_quotes.doc_title: James Moody interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Diego, California, February 13, 1998
